title: Tesla Inc. (TSLA) Stock Outlook Reflects Changing Investor Appetite
detail:
Tesla Inc. (TSLA) Stock Outlook Reflects Changing Investor Appetite
Abdul Rahman
Wed, April 15, 2026 at 9:23 AM CDT 2 min read
TSLA
Trade Tesla on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) is one of the best energy storage stocks to buy according to hedge funds. On April 9, GLJ Research analyst Gordon Johnson reiterated a Sell rating on Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) and maintained his $25.28 price target.

Tesla Inc. (TSLA) Stock Outlook Reflects Changing Investor Appetite

david-von-diemar-ZBWn5DvO0hg-unsplash

Johnson’s main argument is that Tesla’s share price has been inflated for six years not primarily by fundamentals, but by heavy retail call buying that pushed dealers into gamma hedging. As a result, the dealers were forced to buy Tesla stock to offset their exposure, which drove stock price much higher. Analysts describe this pattern as a gamma squeeze.

In 2026, analyst Gordon Johnson argued that Tesla’s stock is no longer being boosted by retail options trading. He pointed out that the put‑to‑call ratio has barely dropped below 0.60 this year, unlike in past years when such moves often triggered gains through dealer hedging. Tesla’s call option activity has also fallen behind the broader S&P 500 every session in 2026, showing weaker investor appetite. Johnson believes this means Tesla’s valuation will now shift away from options‑driven momentum and back toward its actual business fundamentals.

Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) is an electric vehicle and clean energy company. It designs and manufactures battery-based energy storage systems for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications. Its energy storage products include the Powerwall for homes, the Powerpack for commercial use, and the Megapack for large-scale grid projects, all of which store electricity for later use and support grid stability.

While we acknowledge the potential of TSLA as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Prediction: Rivian Stock Could Soar in The Next 5 Years If These 2 Things Happen
detail:
Prediction: Rivian Stock Could Soar in The Next 5 Years If These 2 Things Happen
Prosper Junior Bakiny, The Motley Fool
Wed, April 15, 2026 at 9:20 AM CDT 4 min read
RIVN
NVDA
TSLA
INTC
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Rivian (NASDAQ: RIVN) has established itself as a notable player in the U.S. electric vehicle (EV) market. However, investors who have been with the company since it went public have not gotten their money's worth. Rivian's shares have lost significant value since its IPO. That said, the company could be about to stage a rebound -- in fact, the stock has climbed by 32% over the past 12 months. And if Rivian can execute its strategy over the next half-decade, it could maintain that momentum. Read on to discover two important catalysts that could push Rivian's stock in the right direction through 2031.

Image source: The Motley Fool.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Challenging a giant

In 2025, Tesla's Model Y was the best-selling car in the world -- not just among EVs -- for the third consecutive year. Clearly, the midsize SUV market to which it belongs is a lucrative one. Thus far, Rivian has offered a luxury SUV and a pickup truck to customers. The company is set to launch a more direct competitor to Tesla's Model Y this year. Rivian said its R2 model would begin customer deliveries in the second quarter.

Competing in this niche could significantly expand Rivian's appeal, especially given its somewhat struggling vehicle sales. Total deliveries for the company declined last year, partly due to the expiration of EV tax credits in the U.S toward the end of the year.

The R2 will have a much cheaper starting price than the company's R1S and R1T. That could attract more customers and supercharge Rivian's sales growth. If it can manage to steal a decent share of this market from Tesla over the next five years, the company's revenue could grow at a good clip over this period.

Will Rivian reach level 4?

Rivian is aiming for level 4 autonomy, which would mean its EVs can drive themselves with no human involvement (within certain geographical limits). If it can get there relatively soon -- and do so before Tesla, which has yet to get there -- it will be a big deal for the company. One important reason Rivian needs to achieve level 4 soon is that it signed a deal with Uber Technologies that is contingent on its ability to do so.

Per the terms of the agreement, Uber will purchase 10,000 fully autonomous R2 models to be deployed on its platform across various cities starting in 2028, with an option to buy up to an additional 40,000 models in 2030. Uber will also invest up to $1.25 billion in Rivian, including an upfront payment of $300 million upon regulatory approval of the deal.

title: Tesla Tapes Out AI5 Self-Driving Chip
detail:
Tesla Tapes Out AI5 Self-Driving Chip
Faizan Farooque
Wed, April 15, 2026 at 9:08 AM CDT 1 min read
TSLA
Trade Tesla on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Tesla Inc. (TSLA, Financials) has reached another target in its quest to make self-driving cars. CEO Elon Musk claimed that the company has taped out its AI5 processor. This indicates that the design phase is over and the chip can now be produced.The change is essential since Tesla has been slowly making more of its self-driving technology in-house, from custom silicon to software. The company says that AI5 is helping them turn their ideas for the next generation of self-driving hardware into reality.Tesla is spending a lot of money on the processing power that self-driving cars need. The chip is likely to form a significant element of the company's ambitions for Full Self-Driving cars in the future. He also claimed that people are currently working on new technologies like AI6 and Dojo3. This shows that Tesla is trying to stay ahead of the competition by making cars with greater AI.This revelation is another reminder to investors that Tesla is growing more valuable not only as a car producer but also as a company that builds its own AI and semiconductor ecosystem. The next question is how quickly AI5 can be put to use and whether or not it will genuinely make self-driving better.

title: Tesla Stock in Focus After Musk Reveals AI5 Chip Breakthrough
detail:
Tesla Stock in Focus After Musk Reveals AI5 Chip Breakthrough
Nauman Khan
Wed, April 15, 2026 at 8:35 AM CDT 1 min read
TSLA
005930.KS
TSM
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares are in focus on Wednesday after Chief Executive Elon Musk said the company's AI chip design team had finished work on its AI5 autonomous-driving chip, a step that moves Tesla closer to production, according to a Wednesday post on X from Musk.

Tesla said the chip has completed the design phase, a stage often called tape-out, which means the blueprint is ready for manufacturing. Musk added that AI6, Dojo3 and other chips are also under development, while thanking Samsung Electronics and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing for helping bring AI5 into production.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 6 Warning Signs with TSLA.

Is TSLA fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Tesla has said AI5 is expected to enter high-volume output in 2027 and could replace the current AI4 chip in its vehicles. Musk also said last month that Tesla could tape out its next-generation AI6 chip as early as December.

Tesla shares were up about 1% in early trading. The move comes after Tesla signed a $16.5 billion deal with Samsung last year to make AI6 chips in the U.S., underscoring the company's push to deepen its in-house AI hardware efforts.
